target: Verdict taxonomy structural analysis — all 67 WINs reviewed, Misleading sub-typed, boundary audit, reclassification recommendations
executive_summary: The six-category taxonomy is conceptually sound and should be retained. However, 'Misleading' is overloaded with four distinct failure modes that carry different rhetorical weight; the Misleading/Std Model Explains boundary is inconsistently applied for WINs involving duplication; and two WINs (054, 058) are miscategorized. Recommended actions: (1) Add sub-type labels to the 23 Misleading WINs (data misrepresentation, duplication, non-discriminating, internal derivation, unfalsifiable strategy). (2) Reclassify WIN-054 → Not Demonstrated (already in pending patch ISS-441). (3) Reclassify WIN-058 → Not Demonstrated OR update its finding text to emphasize curve-fitting. (4) Either reclassify WIN-043 and WIN-060 to Misleading for consistency, or remove 'duplicate' language from their findings. (5) Add an explicit decision-rule paragraph to the Evaluation Guide. (6) Add a 'no positive verdict' acknowledgment. (7) Do NOT create a 7th category — the disruption outweighs the benefit; sub-labels achieve the same goal.

misleading_subtype_classification.description: Each of the 23 Misleading WINs classified by PRIMARY failure mode. Some WINs have secondary failure modes noted.

data_misrepresentation.label: Data Misrepresentation
data_misrepresentation.definition: The cited source does not contain what is claimed, values are wrong, units are incorrect, or data is selectively quoted to distort the picture.

wins.primary_issue: 10th harmonic numerically undefined (10×7.83=78.3≠117); conflates acoustic with EM physics
Cherry-picks RMS (6.9 min) from equation-of-time range (+16.5 to -14.1 min)
Units error (rad/s² vs rad/s); rate derived circularly from assumed dome parameters
Presents R²=0.79 as good fit while omitting globe's <0.5% error; 21% unexplained

data_misrepresentation.rhetorical_weight: High — these involve factual errors or selective quotation that can be independently verified. Strongest sub-category for 'Misleading' label.

duplication.label: Duplication / Count Inflation
duplication.definition: The same observation or dataset is counted multiple times as separate 'WINs', inflating the prediction count.

wins.primary_issue: Duplicate of WIN-007 (same NOAA data, same parent prediction PROS-002)
Re-slices same INTERMAGNET data as WIN-004/005/035/037
Mathematically equivalent to WIN-037 (≥28 nT cumulative per 12 months = ≥28 nT/yr rate)

duplication.rhetorical_weight: Medium-high — inflating the count is a structural methodological problem, not necessarily deceptive intent but clearly misleading to readers who see '67 WINs'.

non_discriminating.label: Non-Discriminating / Exploiting Others' Problems
non_discriminating.definition: The prediction does not distinguish the dome from standard physics, OR the dome cites a known problem in ΛCDM without offering any alternative framework. Both models predict the same observation equally well.

wins.primary_issue: Numerical coincidence (loop ratio 2.66) with no causal mechanism proposed
Latitude coincidence; Roaring 40s driven by Coriolis + pressure gradients on both models
Claims low-z Hubble deviations support aetheric redshift but provides no photon-aether equation
False dilemma: ΛCDM anomaly ≠ dome evidence; copper firmament (WIN-034) would block CMB
Hijacks genuine ΛCDM challenge (RAR); dome has no galaxy-scale physics or derivation
Standard candle distances anchored to Gaia parallax; dome has no distance framework beyond 9,086 km

non_discriminating.rhetorical_weight: LOW for implying dishonesty — these WINs don't involve deception. The dome is making a valid (if weak) methodological move: pointing to phenomena that mainstream struggles with. The problem is epistemological (not evidence for dome) rather than ethical (not deceptive). This is the sub-type most likely to alienate fair-minded readers when labeled 'Misleading'.

internal_derivation.label: Internal Derivation / Curve-Fitting / Relabeling
internal_derivation.definition: The 'prediction' is derived from the model's own parameters or fitted to known data, testing self-consistency rather than truth. Includes renaming globe values as dome parameters.

wins.primary_issue: Standard geophysics explains via flux lobe dynamics; dome provides only post-hoc label ('aetheric threshold crossing')
Adding fitted parameters (elliptical disc) always improves fit; no AIC/BIC model comparison
Threshold (≥28 nT) set within established SAA trend guarantees confirmation; input parameter, not output
Internal V12 derivation claimed as confirmed WIN before observational test (eclipse Aug 2026)
Cross-equatorial error reduced by adding fitted parameter (14,105 km ring); curve-fitting 6 known routes
Internal coordinate convention (0.9941 scale factor) fitted to WGS84 distances
Globe circumference (40,075 km) relabeled as disc diameter (40,030 km); numerical agreement by construction

internal_derivation.rhetorical_weight: Medium — curve-fitting and relabeling are methodological critiques that sophisticated readers will understand. Less viscerally 'misleading' than data misrepresentation but more substantive than non-discrimination.

unfalsifiable_strategy.label: Unfalsifiable Strategy / Opposite Outcomes Claimed
unfalsifiable_strategy.definition: The model claims credit for logically contradictory outcomes (both detection and null result), making the prediction unfalsifiable.

wins.primary_issue: Null result at Membach contradicts WIN-011's positive detection at Mohe; claims credit for both
Same logical contradiction as WIN-013 (null result claimed as confirmation alongside positive detection)

unfalsifiable_strategy.rhetorical_weight: High — claiming credit for opposite outcomes is the textbook definition of unfalsifiable reasoning. Note: these could alternatively be classified as 'Unfalsifiable' rather than 'Misleading', but the current placement is defensible because the primary issue is the misleading presentation (claiming credit for both), not the theological untestability.

misleading_subtype_classification.cross_check: 4 + 3 + 6 + 7 + 2 = 22. Note: WIN-054 (currently counted as Misleading) is excluded because it should be reclassified to 'Not Demonstrated' (pending patch ISS-441). With WIN-054 reclassified, the Misleading total becomes 22.

misleading_vs_std_model_explains.stated_rule: None explicitly stated. The definitions are: Misleading = 'data is misrepresented, cherry-picked, cited values don't match source, or contradictory results both claimed as confirmations.' Std Model Explains = 'observation is real but mainstream physics provides complete quantitative explanation; dome adds no predictive power.'
misleading_vs_std_model_explains.implicit_rule: In practice, the boundary appears to be: if the dome adds manipulation (cherry-picking, renaming, duplication, wrong citations) ON TOP of non-discrimination, it gets Misleading. If the dome simply claims a real observation that standard physics already explains, with no additional manipulation, it gets Std Model Explains.

inconsistencies.issue: Finding text says 'duplicate of WIN-007' — duplication is a Misleading criterion per the implicit rule. The primary argument is that the 2.26× ratio is arithmetic from NOAA data, which IS a 'Std Model Explains' argument. But WIN-041/042 receive 'Misleading' for similar duplication of geomagnetic data.
inconsistencies.recommendation: Remove 'duplicate of WIN-007' from finding text OR reclassify to Misleading. The primary failure is that the ratio is arithmetic from shared data (non-discriminating), which supports 'Std Model Explains'. Removing the duplication language is cleaner — the duplicative aspect is secondary to the non-discrimination.

inconsistencies.issue: Finding text says 'duplicate of WIN-004/005' — same inconsistency as WIN-043. WIN-041 is 'Misleading' for re-slicing the same SAA data, but WIN-060 is 'Std Model Explains' for duplicating SAA drift tracking.
inconsistencies.recommendation: Same as WIN-043: either remove duplication language from finding or reclassify. For consistency with WIN-060's primary argument (non-discriminating extrapolation), prefer keeping 'Std Model Explains' and softening the finding to: 'Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same drift data as NOAA/ESA; adds no unique prediction from dome geometry.'

inconsistencies.wins_in_both_camps: 7 Std Model Explains WINs use 'non-discriminating' in their findings (006, 010, 020, 035, 036, 040, 060). 6 Misleading WINs are also primarily non-discriminating (019, 024, 047, 048, 052, 055). The distinction is that the 6 Misleading non-discriminating WINs have ADDITIONAL problems: 019 has no mechanism at all (pure coincidence), 024 is a latitude coincidence, 047/052/055 have no dome-specific physics, 048 adds a logical fallacy (false dilemma). These additional problems justify 'Misleading' over 'Std Model Explains' — but this decision rule needs to be stated.
inconsistencies.proposed_decision_rule: If the PRIMARY failure is that standard physics explains the observation and the dome merely claims credit without additional manipulation or logical error, use 'Std Model Explains.' If the dome introduces additional distortion — cherry-picking, fabrication, false dilemma, count inflation, or presenting an absence of dome physics as a positive prediction — use 'Misleading.' State this rule in the Evaluation Guide.

misleading_vs_not_demonstrated.issue: WIN-054 and WIN-058 straddle this boundary. 'Misleading' definition focuses on data manipulation; 'Not Demonstrated' focuses on unverified evidence. Neither definition cleanly captures 'dome hasn't done the work to turn this into a real prediction.'
misleading_vs_not_demonstrated.proposed_expansion: Broaden 'Not Demonstrated' to: 'The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments, unverified data, or asserts a prediction without derivation from the model's geometry or any observational confirmation.' This absorbs WIN-054 naturally and makes WIN-058 a candidate.

specific_reclassifications.rationale: El Gordo is a genuine 6.2σ ΛCDM anomaly documented in peer-reviewed literature. The dome is not misrepresenting data. The failure is that the dome cites an opponent's problem without offering any cosmological structure formation model, collision velocity prediction, or derivation from dome geometry. This is textbook 'Not Demonstrated' — the dome has identified a real open question but has not demonstrated that its model addresses it. A dome defender reading 'Misleading' here would correctly object: the 6.2σ is in the literature.
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: El Gordo is a genuine 6.2σ anomaly for ΛCDM, but the dome has no cosmological model, no structure formation equations, and no alternative prediction for cluster collision velocities. Citing an opponent's anomaly is not a prediction — it is an absence of work.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: El Gordo IS a real tension in ΛCDM. The dome correctly identifies it. But identifying a problem in the competition is not the same as solving it.

specific_reclassifications.rationale: The curmudgeon gave 0.60 confidence for reclassifying to 'Not Demonstrated.' On deeper analysis, the detail_verdict_text reveals genuine curve-fitting: the 0.9941 scale factor is fitted to WGS84 distances. This IS misleading — presenting a curve-fit to globe data as a dome 'prediction.' The problem is that the one-liner finding ('Internal coordinate convention, not a physical prediction') doesn't convey this. Fix the finding text, not the verdict.
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: Post-hoc fit of 0.9941 scale factor to WGS84 distances; fitting known data derived from globe geodesy is not prediction. The coordinate system is a convention, not a testable claim.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: Constructing a unified coordinate system is legitimate mathematical work. The dome's angular-to-linear mapping is internally consistent. But internal consistency is not confirmation — any fitted coordinate system will be self-consistent.

specific_reclassifications.recommended_verdict: Std Model Explains (retain, with updated finding text)
specific_reclassifications.rationale: The primary failure is non-discrimination: the 2.26× longitudinal-to-latitudinal drift ratio is arithmetic from NOAA trajectory data, derivable by anyone with the data. Standard core dynamics (Livermore 2020) explains the drift. The 'duplicate of WIN-007' mention is secondary and creates an inconsistency with WIN-041/042. Resolution: remove 'duplicate' from finding text rather than reclassify, because the primary argument is genuinely 'Std Model Explains.'
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: Non-discriminating: 2.26× ratio is arithmetic from NOAA trajectory data, not derived from dome geometry. Standard core dynamics (Livermore 2020) explains the directional asymmetry via competing flux lobes.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: The 2.26× ratio is a real feature of the NMP trajectory. The dome correctly identifies it. But computing a ratio from shared data and claiming it as a 'prediction' is not science.

specific_reclassifications.recommended_verdict: Std Model Explains (retain, with updated finding text)
specific_reclassifications.rationale: Same logic as WIN-043. The primary failure is non-discrimination (dome extrapolates same drift data as NOAA/ESA). The 'duplicate of WIN-004/005' mention is secondary. Remove duplication language for consistency.
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same SAA drift data as NOAA/ESA; adds no unique prediction derived from dome geometry.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: The SAA western cell IS drifting westward. This is real and well-documented. Both models track the same data.

specific_reclassifications.rationale: The curmudgeon raised this as a candidate for 'Refuted by Data' since P-wave shadow zone geometry PROVES a spherical layered Earth. However, the dome doesn't make a specific shadow-zone prediction that contradicts data — it simply claims the observation without derivation. 'Refuted by Data' requires the dome to have stated something that data contradicts. The dome's claim is vacuous (no physics offered), which is different from wrong. 'Std Model Explains' correctly captures that standard seismology provides the complete explanation. Consider strengthening finding text.
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: Shadow zone at 104-140° requires spherical layered Earth with liquid core; no flat-disc geometry can produce this angular refraction pattern. Standard seismology explains completely.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: The P-wave shadow zone IS a powerful observation. The dome correctly identifies it as important geophysics. But it proves the Earth is spherical with concentric shells — the opposite of the dome's claim.

specific_reclassifications.rationale: The geophysical timing claim (~950 AD) is testable and well-studied via archaeomagnetic records and lake sediment cores. The dome's theological overlay (linking to a religious timeline) is unfalsifiable. Since we can only assign one verdict, 'Unfalsifiable' captures the more important critique — the theological framing is what makes this claim pseudo-scientific. But the finding text should acknowledge the kernel of truth: the geomagnetic event around that period is real.
specific_reclassifications.finding_text_update: The SAA's intensification around ~950 AD is supported by archaeomagnetic records and explained by core dynamics — this timing is real geophysics, not a dome prediction. The theological interpretation (linking to a specific religious narrative) is the unfalsifiable component and is what elevates an observation to a 'WIN' in the dome framework.
specific_reclassifications.kernel_of_truth: There IS geomagnetic evidence for significant field changes in the ~900-1000 AD period. The dome correctly identifies the approximate timing. Standard geophysics explains it via core convection patterns without any theological overlay.

seventh_category_analysis.definition: The prediction does not distinguish the dome model from standard physics. Both models predict the same observation equally well. The dome adds no unique predictive content.

wins_that_would_move.note: WIN-047/048/052/055 are non-discriminating but have ADDITIONAL problems (false dilemma, no mechanism, exploiting ΛCDM issues) that justify keeping them in Misleading.

arguments_for: Separates methodological irrelevance (non-discriminating) from deception (misleading) — fairer to dome author
13 WINs across two categories share this primary failure mode — deserves explicit recognition
Aligns with philosophy of science: Lakatos's distinction between 'novel facts' (genuine predictions) and 'accommodated facts' (known observations claimed post-hoc)
Removes the most exploitable ambiguity: dome defender can't claim we're calling real observations 'misleading'
arguments_against: 7 categories is harder for readers to track than 6
Moving WINs between categories mid-review creates credibility risk ('they keep changing their answers')
'Std Model Explains' already IMPLIES non-discrimination — the distinction is razor-thin
Massive disruption: reclassify ~9 WINs, update all HTML/DOCX, add new color, rebuild pie chart
The boundary between 'Std Model Explains' and 'Non-Discriminating' would itself be porous — same problem we're trying to solve
Most readers don't need this level of taxonomic granularity — they need to see that 0/67 WINs genuinely outpredict standard physics
seventh_category_analysis.recommendation: DO NOT CREATE A 7TH CATEGORY. The disruption outweighs the benefit. Instead: (1) Add sub-type labels to Misleading WINs in the detail writeups (a parenthetical like '[cherry-picked]' or '[non-discriminating]' after the verdict in the detail view). (2) Add a paragraph to the Evaluation Guide explicitly stating that 'Non-Discriminating' is a failure mode that appears in both 'Std Model Explains' and 'Misleading' categories, distinguished by whether additional manipulation is present. (3) If the dome defender's process objection becomes a real rhetorical problem in the future, revisit then — don't pre-optimize for an objection that hasn't been raised.

no_positive_verdict_problem.description: The curmudgeon correctly identifies that there is no 'Dome Correct' or 'Dome Outpredicts' category. Four of six verdicts are negative, one is neutral (Unfalsifiable), and one acknowledges real observations (Std Model Explains). A dome defender would argue the taxonomy is rigged from the start — designed to produce only negative outcomes.
no_positive_verdict_problem.why_it_matters: This is a legitimate methodological objection. If we found a WIN where the dome genuinely predicted something that standard physics could not, what verdict would we assign? The absence of a positive category reveals an assumption that the dome model is wrong, which is exactly what the review sets out to prove. This is methodological circularity.
no_positive_verdict_problem.why_it_doesnt_save_the_dome: We could add a 'Dome Outpredicts' category — and no WIN would receive it. The absence of positive verdicts is a FINDING, not an ASSUMPTION. But we need to make this explicit, or the circularity objection stands.
no_positive_verdict_problem.recommended_action: Add a paragraph to the Evaluation Guide acknowledging this: 'Our taxonomy includes no positive verdict because no WIN we examined genuinely outpredicted standard physics. We would create such a category if a WIN met the discrimination criterion (Part 1.5, Principle 4): the dome must predict a specific, quantitative result that differs from the standard model prediction AND that observation must match the dome's prediction. We searched for this among all 67 WINs and did not find it. The August 2026 eclipse prediction (WIN-044) is the closest candidate — see Kill-Shot Test 5.' This transforms a potential process objection into a powerful argument: we looked for the dome to be right and it wasn't.
no_positive_verdict_problem.kernel_of_truth: The dome defender is right that our taxonomy assumes the dome is wrong. But the assumption is justified by the data — every WIN, when examined, fails to outpredict standard physics. Making this explicit is stronger than leaving it implicit.

self_contradicted_tidal_group_note.description: The curmudgeon flags that five tidal WINs (045-051) share a single geometric argument, inflating the Self-Contradicted count from 8 independent arguments to 12 listed WINs — the same count inflation we criticize the dome for.
self_contradicted_tidal_group_note.recommendation: Add a transparency note in the Self-Contradicted section summary: 'Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) share a single root geometric contradiction: the dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude produces a sharp tidal spike beneath it, not the observed two-bulge semidiurnal pattern. Each is individually self-contradicted, but they represent one independent argument counted against five separate predictions — a structural feature we also identify in the dome's own prediction counting (see Section 3.5.3).' This preempts the irony objection while maintaining honesty.
self_contradicted_tidal_group_note.note: The detail_group field already connects these WINs. The HTML groups them visually. But the summary count (12 Self-Contradicted) does not acknowledge the shared root. A brief note is sufficient.

verdict_legend_reordering.rationale: Group by evidential force: (1) Wrong on own terms: Self-Contradicted (strongest — dome refutes itself), Refuted by Data (strong — external data contradicts). (2) Not evidence for dome: Std Model Explains, Misleading. (3) Not evaluable: Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable. Leading with Self-Contradicted sends the right rhetorical signal: the strongest arguments are the ones where the dome defeats itself, requiring no appeal to external authority.
verdict_legend_reordering.implementation: Change VERDICT_ORDER array in generate-html.js. Minor change, no data impact.

proposed_evaluation_guide_additions.decision_rule_paragraph: How verdicts are assigned: Each WIN receives the verdict that captures its primary failure mode. When a WIN has multiple problems, we use the most specific applicable verdict. For example, a WIN that both (a) relies on standard physics and (b) cherry-picks data receives 'Misleading' rather than 'Std Model Explains,' because the data manipulation is a more specific and more damaging critique. A WIN that simply claims a real observation without any added distortion receives 'Std Model Explains.' WINs labeled 'Misleading' span several distinct failure modes — from factual errors in cited sources to count inflation to curve-fitting known data — and the detail writeup for each WIN specifies which type of problem applies.
proposed_evaluation_guide_additions.no_positive_verdict_paragraph: Why is there no positive verdict? Our taxonomy has no 'Dome Outpredicts Standard Model' category because no WIN we examined meets the discrimination criterion stated in Principle 4: the dome must predict a specific, quantitative result that differs from the standard model prediction, and the observation must match the dome's number rather than the standard number. We searched all 67 WINs for this and found zero cases. If the August 2026 eclipse prediction (WIN-044, Kill-Shot Test 5) produces a magnetic signal in the dome's predicted range (−17 to −21 nT) and outside the Chapman range (5–15 nT), we will create such a category and assign it. The absence of a positive verdict is a finding of the review, not an assumption built into the taxonomy.
proposed_evaluation_guide_additions.non_discrimination_note: Non-Discriminating predictions: Many WINs involve observations that are real but predicted equally well by both models. These appear under either 'Std Model Explains' (when the dome simply claims a known observation without additional manipulation) or 'Misleading' (when the dome also adds a false dilemma, cherry-picks data, or imports terminology without mechanism). A prediction that both models make equally well is not evidence for either — this is a standard principle from philosophy of science (Lakatos's criterion of 'novel facts').

not_demonstrated_definition_expansion.current: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed.
not_demonstrated_definition_expansion.proposed: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments, unverified data, or asserts a prediction without derivation from the model's geometry or observational confirmation. The dome has not demonstrated that its framework produces the claimed result.
not_demonstrated_definition_expansion.rationale: The current definition is too narrow — it only covers fringe experiments (WIN-011, 012, 015). The expanded definition also captures WINs where the dome simply claims a phenomenon without any derivation or framework. This makes WIN-054 a natural fit and keeps the door open for WIN-058 if the Misleading classification is reconsidered later.
not_demonstrated_definition_expansion.impact: No reclassifications beyond WIN-054 are required by this expansion. It's a defensive expansion — it makes the category definition match its intended use.

code_analysis_cross_tabulation.description: The curmudgeon suggested a cross-reference table showing code_analysis tags by verdict. This strengthens both the Part 4.6 and verdict taxonomy sections.

table.note: Based on 31 reviewed WINs (code_analysis.reviewed=true). Tags from wins.json.
table.insight: Hardcoded monitoring correlates strongly with Misleading and Refuted verdicts — WINs with hardcoded checks tend to have weaker underlying science. Live-fetch WINs cluster in Std Model Explains, suggesting the dome's monitoring is actually more rigorous for claims that happen to be non-discriminating (real observations that standard physics also explains).

implementation_priority.action: Update WIN-058 finding text to emphasize curve-fitting
Update WIN-043 finding text to remove 'duplicate' language
Update WIN-060 finding text to remove 'duplicate' language
Update WIN-023 finding text to acknowledge kernel of truth
Update WIN-064 finding text to strengthen
Expand Not Demonstrated definition in generate-html.js
Add decision-rule paragraph to Evaluation Guide
Add no-positive-verdict paragraph to Evaluation Guide
Add non-discrimination note to Evaluation Guide
Add tidal group transparency note to Self-Contradicted section
Add sub-type labels to Misleading detail writeups
implementation_priority.effort: 23 edits to wins.json detail fields — defer to future pass

holes_addressed: ISS-442: Misleading overload — classified all 23 WINs into 5 sub-types with counts and rhetorical weight assessment
ISS-443: WIN-043/060 boundary inconsistency — recommended finding text updates to remove duplication language, maintaining Std Model Explains verdicts
ISS-444: WIN-058 verdict mismatch — recommended retaining Misleading with updated finding text emphasizing curve-fitting
ISS-447: Verdict legend ordering — proposed reorder with Self-Contradicted first (low priority)
ISS-448: Not Demonstrated definition incomplete — proposed expanded definition covering 'no derivation from model geometry'
kernel_of_truth: The six-category taxonomy captures genuine diversity that simpler taxonomies (right/wrong, confirmed/refuted) would miss. The existence of 'Std Model Explains' as distinct from 'Refuted' is particularly strong — it acknowledges observations are real while denying they constitute evidence. This nuance is what distinguishes a serious analysis from a debunking screed. The dome defender's strongest objection — that there's no positive category — is methodologically legitimate and should be addressed head-on rather than ignored.
